sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Cagliari are struggling, winless in their last 10 matches, with recent results showing draws and losses (1-1 vs Napoli, 3-3 vs Genoa). Roma, in contrast, have shown strong form with 3 wins in their last 5 matches, including a 3-1 victory over Cremonese and a 2-0 win against Udinese, though they lost 0-1 to Napoli recently.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Roma have the upper hand, securing a 1-0 win in March 2025 and a 0-0 draw in August 2024. Roma have not lost to Cagliari in their last 6 meetings, often controlling possession and creating more chances.

sections.subtitle: Team Strengths and Weaknesses
sections.text: Roma, sitting 4th with 27 points, boast a solid attack led by Matías Soulé (4 goals) and a strong defense (only 7 goals conceded). Cagliari, 15th with 11 points, struggle defensively (19 goals conceded) and lack creativity in the final third, despite decent conversion of big chances (52.17%).

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Roma, Lorenzo Pellegrini has been a standout against Cagliari historically (7.68 avg. rating, 2 goals, 2 assists). Cagliari will rely on forwards Gennaro Borrelli and Sebastiano Esposito, both with 3 goals this season, to break through Roma’s defense.

sections.subtitle: Statistical Insights
sections.text: Roma tend to dominate possession (avg. 57% in recent H2H) and are often first to score (4/5 in last 5 away games). Cagliari commit many fouls (211 this season, 3rd in league), which could lead to disciplinary issues against Roma’s disciplined side.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Roma’s superior form, league position, and historical edge, they are slight favorites per Sofascore’s probabilities (37% away win vs 34% home win). Cagliari’s home struggles and inability to win recently tilt the balance towards Roma, though a draw (29%) remains plausible.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, the match between Cagliari and Roma stands at a goalless draw. Neither side has managed to break the deadlock despite some promising moments. Roma have dominated possession and shown more presence in the attacking third with a higher number of entries into the final third and penalty area. However, Cagliari have been more clinical in creating significant opportunities, evidenced by their creation of a big chance, though it was missed. Both teams have shown defensive resilience, with no major disciplinary issues impacting the game so far.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating momentum between the two sides. Roma started strongly with an early push, maintaining pressure in the initial minutes. Cagliari responded with a sustained period of attacking intent, especially in the middle of the half, where they had several spells of dominance in the opposition's half. Towards the latter stages, Roma regained some control with consistent forward movements, while Cagliari still managed occasional bursts of pressure. The momentum shifts suggest a closely contested battle with both teams capable of taking the initiative.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key player performances
halftimeSections.text: For Cagliari, Marco Palestra has been a standout in creating opportunities, showing creativity with key passes and involvement in a big chance created. Sebastiano Esposito and Michael Folorunsho have also been threats in front of goal, testing Roma’s defense. On the Roma side, Matías Soulé has been influential in the attacking third, contributing with key passes and a shot on target. Players like Bryan Cristante and Manu Koné have provided stability in midfield, helping Roma maintain their possession advantage. Both teams have players who can turn the game in their favor in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical insights
halftimeSections.text: Cagliari seem to be focusing on quick transitions, looking to exploit spaces in Roma’s defense, as seen in their penalty area entries and shots on target. However, their finishing has let them down with a missed big chance. Roma, on the other hand, have adopted a more possession-based approach, patiently building attacks with a high number of final third entries. Their lack of big chances created indicates a need for more incisiveness in the final third. Defensively, both teams have been solid, with Cagliari slightly edging out in duels won, while Roma have a marginal advantage in aerial battles.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: The second half is likely to see an increase in intensity as both teams push for a breakthrough. Roma may look to capitalize on their possession dominance by creating clearer chances, potentially bringing in fresh legs to add dynamism. Cagliari will aim to convert their opportunities, focusing on clinical finishing to punish any defensive lapses from Roma. Given the balance of play in the first half, a goal could come from either side, but Roma’s control of the ball might give them a slight edge if they can sharpen their attack. Defensive organization will be crucial for both teams to avoid conceding on the counter.
